0:845.name: Type=Square, Size=Medium, Style=Filled
0:845.description:
Standard Icon buttons are low-emphasis buttons.
They are used for the lowest priority actions, especially when presenting multiple options.

Icon buttons can be placed on a variety of backgrounds. Until the button is interacted with, its container isn’t visible.

16:3912.name: Icon Button/Standard (OnSurface)/Enabled
16:3912.description:
Filled buttons are used for final or unblocking actions in a flow

High emphasis – For the primary, most important, or most common action on a screen

16:3910.description: arrow, arrows, direction, left, android, back, previous

8:2494.name: Horizontal/Full-width
8:2494.description:
Dividers are one way to visually group components and create hierarchy. They can also be used to imply nested parent/child relationships.

Use full width dividers to separate larger sections of unrelated content. They can be used directly on a surface or inside other components like cards or lists.

0:760.name: Button
0:760.description:
Buttons communicate actions that users can take.


Can contain an optional leading icon
Five color options: elevated, filled, tonal, outlined, and text
Five size recommendations: extra small, small, medium, large, extra large
Two shape options: round and square
Keep labels concise and use sentence-case

0:58.name: Icon Button
0:58.description: Icon buttons help people take supplementary actions with a single tap. They’re used when a compact button is required, such as in a toolbar or image list.

39:6831.name: List item/List Item: -2 Density (baseline)
39:6831.description:
List items can contain primary and supplemental actions represented by text, a leading icon and/or a trailing icon

Density can be used in denser UIs where space is limited. Density is only applied to the height.

🛠 Additional options 
List item - can be edited by selecting an individual list item and  selecting states, leading icons, trailing icons, and dividers from the variant properties.
